backend: stateful synthetic mock using the production PTO policy module
privacy: synthetic TEST data only; no live identities, dates, balances, notes, keys, or responses

shots.expected_visible_result: The signed-in staff member sees an enabled balance, request form, own history, team snapshot, and calendar.
The custom request-type list opens with clear colored options and no native browser dropdown.
Unpaid becomes the selected branded option and the menu closes.
The SyncView calendar opens with month navigation, weekday headers, and a clear date grid.
The selected start date is shown in the branded field and the calendar closes.
The end-date calendar opens in SyncView style with dates before the selected start disabled.
Choosing the end date closes the calendar and the branded day stepper shows the local business-day total.
The down-arrow changes the count by one half-day and keeps the control visually coherent.

shots.action: restore the full quoted day count
shots.expected_visible_result: The up-arrow restores the original full-day count without sending a request.
The note field visibly accepts plain-language context without submitting a request.
The note field returns to a clean empty draft without changing PTO state.
The form waits for the authenticated server quote, then shows a real business-day total without creating a request.
The request appears as Pending in My requests while the visible wellness balance stays unchanged.
Kasper sees the submitted request with decision-note, Approve, and Deny controls.
Kasper can add a plain-English decision note before taking action.
The request leaves Pending, appears in Upcoming Approved Leave, and the member balance visibly decreases.
The staff member sees Approved with the decision note and a lower wellness balance.
The approved future leave is visible on the shared team calendar and the Team snapshot shows the updated availability.
Kasper sees a clear confirmation explaining that the calendar block will be removed while history remains.
The approved block disappears from Upcoming Leave and history preserves both approval and cancellation attribution.
The wellness balance is restored and the cancelled block is gone from the team calendar.
Sick leave accepts the past business date and appears Pending without deducting the sick balance.
Kasper approves with a note and the visible sick balance decreases after the decision.
The sick request is Approved with its note and the sick-days metric visibly reads two.
The unpaid request appears Pending and neither paid balance changes.
Kasper can enter the reason for a denial while Approve and Deny remain clearly distinct.
The request moves to Recent Decisions as Denied, the note is retained, and balances stay unchanged.
The first floating holiday becomes Pending and visibly reserves the annual allowance.

shots.action: stale tab tries a second floating holiday
shots.expected_visible_result: The server rejects the stale second request in plain English; no duplicate row is created.
Kasper approves the reserved floating holiday and it moves to Upcoming Leave.
The floating metric reads Used and the approved day renders on the calendar.
A half-day wellness request appears Pending and does not yet reduce the balance.
The staff member sees a clear confirmation before the pending request is cancelled.
The pending request becomes Cancelled and the balance/calendar remain unchanged.
The request is rejected in plain English, the form remains usable, and no row is created.
A second creative-role persona sees only their own request history and submits one pending unpaid request.
Kasper sees a plain-English inactive-profile error and the pending card remains available for cleanup.
Kasper can deny the inactive member request and Recent Decisions accumulates the terminal row.
A temporary server failure appears in the form and Send request is enabled for an explicit retry.
The unchanged form retries successfully and the pending row appears once.

shots.action: connection drop locks unknown write outcome
shots.expected_visible_result: A dropped mutating connection says SyncView could not confirm the save and requires Refresh before another submission.
Refresh confirms that no request was saved, clears the safety lock, and restores Send request.
Even when the server saved the row before the response disappeared, the UI locks and does not invite a duplicate retry.

shots.action: refresh finds the saved request once
shots.expected_visible_result: Refresh reconciles the uncertain result and reveals exactly one Pending row without resubmission.

shots.action: slow request stays visibly in flight
shots.expected_visible_result: A genuinely delayed response keeps Send disabled with an in-progress label and does not create an early row.
The delayed response finishes normally and produces exactly one Pending request.

shots.action: hung request times out into safe lock
shots.expected_visible_result: A request that outlives the client timeout becomes an unknown write outcome and remains locked until Refresh.
A successful read confirms no row and restores the request form without writing anything.
A human double-click produces one API call and one pending request, with no duplicate row.
The first tab shows the new pending request while the already-open second tab stays stale.
The second tab explicitly refreshes and converges on the new pending request.
The second tab cancels the request; the first tab remains stale until its own Refresh.
The first tab converges on the cancelled state after an explicit Refresh.

shots.action: sign out with a draft in progress
shots.expected_visible_result: Signing out immediately removes the private PTO data and the unsent draft from every open tab.

shots.action: sign in as second same-role persona
shots.expected_visible_result: A different same-role staff member signs in and receives only their own fresh overview; the first draft is gone.
Refresh makes the policy-date balance transition visible using the production accrual engine.
Refresh makes the policy-date balance transition visible using the production accrual engine.
Refresh makes the policy-date balance transition visible using the production accrual engine.
Refresh makes the policy-date balance transition visible using the production accrual engine.
Refresh makes the policy-date balance transition visible using the production accrual engine.
Refresh makes the policy-date balance transition visible using the production accrual engine.
Refresh makes the policy-date balance transition visible using the production accrual engine.

shots.action: Guatemala 18:00 stays on the local policy date
shots.expected_visible_result: The server policy date keeps the request minimum and calendar Today marker on the Guatemala business day.

shots.action: Guatemala 23:59 stays on the local policy date
shots.expected_visible_result: The server policy date keeps the request minimum and calendar Today marker on the Guatemala business day.

shots.action: Guatemala midnight advances the policy date
shots.expected_visible_result: The server policy date keeps the request minimum and calendar Today marker on the Guatemala business day.

shots.action: tap open the mobile staff menu
shots.expected_visible_result: The staff menu opens beside its trigger and the Time Off item is visible inside the phone viewport.

shots.action: tap Time Off in the mobile staff menu
shots.expected_visible_result: The balance, request form, history, and scrollable calendar fit the mobile viewport without page overflow.

shots.action: tap open the mobile request-type menu
shots.expected_visible_result: The branded type menu opens inside the 390px viewport without a native picker or clipping.
Unpaid is selected and the touch menu closes cleanly.

shots.action: tap open the mobile date calendar
shots.expected_visible_result: The branded calendar opens as a touch-friendly, viewport-contained panel.

shots.action: tap the mobile calendar next-month arrow
shots.expected_visible_result: The calendar advances one month and keeps its touch target and layout visible.
The selected start day closes the calendar and appears in the branded field.

shots.action: tap open the mobile end-date calendar
shots.expected_visible_result: The end-date calendar opens in May with earlier dates disabled and remains inside the phone viewport.

shots.action: tap the matching mobile end date
shots.expected_visible_result: The matching end date closes the calendar and produces the exact one-day quote.
The touch-friendly down arrow visibly changes the one-day quote to a half day.
The touch-friendly up arrow visibly restores the full one-day quote.
The mobile note field accepts the request context without horizontal overflow.
The branded mobile controls submit one unpaid request and the Pending row is readable without native widgets.
Kasper reviews and approves the mobile request with controls contained at 390px.
After Refresh, the mobile history shows Approved and unpaid leave leaves paid balances unchanged.

shots.action: keyboard enters Time Off and reaches request type
shots.expected_visible_result: Tab and Enter open the staff menu route, then natural tab order reaches the request-type control with a visible focus ring.
Enter opens the branded option list and keeps focus inside the custom control.
End moves the visible focus ring to the Unpaid option without selecting it prematurely.
Enter selects Unpaid and closes the branded list.
Natural Tab order reaches the branded start-date button with a visible focus ring.
Enter opens the branded calendar and visibly focuses a date.
Arrow/Page navigation and Enter select the exact start date and close the calendar.
Natural Tab order reaches the branded end-date button with a visible focus ring.
Enter opens the end-date calendar without losing visible keyboard focus.
Enter chooses the exact end date and the one-day quote becomes available.
Natural Tab order reaches the note field with a visible focus ring.
Typing visibly fills the note field without moving focus.
Natural Tab order reaches Send request with a visible focus ring.
Enter sends exactly one request and lands on a visible Pending row.
Tab and Enter open Kasper Time Off, then natural tab order reaches the exact request note with visible focus.
Typing visibly fills the exact Kasper decision-note field without changing state.
Tab reaches Approve with a visible focus ring and does not decide yet.
A second Tab reaches the distinct Deny action with a visible focus ring.
Enter saves exactly one denial and the Recent Decisions count updates.
Tab and Enter expand Recent Decisions and reveal the exact denied request and note.
After a keyboard-activated Refresh, the staff history shows the Denied result and decision note.
